title: ranunculus quadrivaginatus
intro: Ranunculus quadrivaginatus, also known as the four-seeded ranunculid, is a flowering plant species within the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of ranunculin, a toxic compound. Ranunculus quadrivaginatus is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and pain, as well as treating skin conditions and wounds. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
